citations.narrative:
One Rhabdomys pumilio mouse was not handled as carefully as possible by investigator staff during cage change on
7/28/25. The adult mice and their 7-9 day old litter were transferred to a clean cage, and the dirty cage/bedding was then
processed for sanitization. After the enclosure was processed, technicians found one pup was deceased. Veterinary staff
were immediately contacted and concluded that the pup was already dead when the adults and the litter were transferred
to the clean enclosure. This conclusion was based on the physical appearance of the pup and the more rapid
development of this species of mice after birth; 7-9 day old Rhabdomys pups are active, have open eyes, are fully furred,
and behave similarly to an adult.
Per this Section, the handling of animals by all personnel should be done as carefully as possible in a manner that does
not cause physical harm or unnecessary discomfort. The deceased Rhabdomys pup was not handled as carefully as
possible during cage transfer when investigator staff did not thoroughly check the bedding of the dirty cage prior to the
cage being processed for sanitization. This incident was voluntarily reported to USDA and corrective measures were
implemented prior to this inspection, which included the retraining of investigator and animal care staff on the Rhabdomys
dirty cage change procedure and modifying the procedures for changing and processing dirty cages by incorporating
additional checks at each phase of the procedures. No additional incidents have occurred.
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representatives.
*END OF REPORT*
n

narrative:
2.38(f)(1)  Repeat
   
Miscellaneous. 
One Rhabdomys pumilio mouse was not handled as carefully as possible by investigator staff during cage change on 
7/28/25. The adult mice and their 7-9 day old litter were transferred to a clean cage, and the dirty cage/bedding was then

development of this species of mice after birth; 7-9 day old Rhabdomys pups are active, have open eyes, are fully furred, 
and behave similarly to an adult.  
 
Per this Section, the handling of animals by all personnel should be done as carefully as possible in a manner that does 
not cause physical harm or unnecessary discomfort. The deceased Rhabdomys pup was not handled as carefully as 
possible during cage transfer when investigator staff did not thoroughly check the bedding of the dirty cage prior to the

dirty cage change procedure and modifying the procedures for changing and processing dirty cages by incorporating 
additional checks at each phase of the procedures. No additional incidents have occurred. 
 
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representatives.  
 
*END OF REPORT*
